tavily.answer: Recent developments in Chad's Lac province highlight ongoing security and humanitarian challenges. Conflict and instability have exacerbated the humanitarian situation, with reports of abuse and exploitation by some staff members of Doctors Without Borders in areas along the Sudanese border. The organization's internal investigation found cases of sexual exploitation targeting underage girls and instances of trading food or jobs for sex with refugees. This has led to the dismissal and barring of 18 staff members from future employment. The situation underscores the difficulties in providing aid amidst conflict, with ongoing efforts to address both security and humanitarian needs.

items.title: DRC Ebola hospital set on fire as protesters demand access to bodies of dead relatives - africanews.com
items.snippet: **Violence has erupted in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province after tensions linked to the handling of victims of the latest Ebola outbreak.**. Protesters in the town of Rwampara wanted to recover the bodies of relatives who died of Ebola in order to bury them.
